steps.title: Use the rectangle diagonal
steps.content: A rectangle's diagonal and two adjacent sides form a right triangle. Use the Pythagorean theorem with hypotenuse $6\sqrt{17}$, shorter leg $6$, and unknown longer leg $x$.

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem for the rectangle's diagonal
steps.content:
In a rectangle, the diagonal and two adjacent sides form a right triangle. With diagonal $6\sqrt{17}$, shorter side $6$, and unknown longer side $x$:
$$(6\sqrt{17})^2 = 6^2 + x^2$$

steps.title: Solve for the longer side
steps.content:
Expand the left side: $(6\sqrt{17})^2 = 36 \cdot 17 = 612$
$$612 = 36 + x^2$$
$$x^2 = 576$$
$$x = 24$$
Since $24 \gt 6$, this is the longer side. That is the correct answer.
